data.title: BBVA Argentina Announces Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results Schedule
data.description:
BUENOS AIRES, Argentina--(BUSINESS WIRE)--February 19, 2026--

Banco BBVA Argentina S.A. (NYSE; BYMA; MAE: BBAR; LATIBEX: XBBAR) today announced that it will report its Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 results.

Earnings Release

Wednesday, March 4, 2026

Time: After market close

Conference Call

Thursday, March 5, 2026

Time: 12:00 p.m. Buenos Aires time — (10:00 a.m. EST)

Quiet Period

From Wednesday, February 18, through Wednesday, March 4, 2026

Executives

Ms. Carmen Morillo Arroyo, Chief Financial Officer

Mr. Diego Cesarini, Head of ALM & Investor Relations

Ms. Belén Fourcade, Investor Relations Manager

To participate, please click here to register

About BBVA Argentina

BBVA Argentina S.A. (NYSE; MAE; BYMA: BBAR; Latibex: XBBAR) is a subsidiary of the BBVA Group, its main shareholder since 1996. In Argentina, it has been one of the leading financial institutions since 1886. BBVA Argentina offers retail and corporate banking to a wide client base, including individuals, SMEs, and large corporations.

BBVA's strategy is to support its clients' ambition to go further. This is achieved through constant and empathetic support during key moments, recognizing the inner strength that drives people. The value proposition focuses on anticipation and innovation to be the ideal partner that helps clients reach their goals.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260218050097/en/

CONTACT: BBVA Argentina Investor Relations

data.title: Canada Directs Financial-Intelligence Agency to Shift Resources to Combat Extortion
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canada has directed the country's financial-intelligence agency to immediately shift resources toward working with law-enforcement authorities and banks to disrupt extortion.

Finance Minister Francois-Philippe Champagne said the agency, known as the Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada, must take a leading role in disrupting the illicit financing that fuels extortion, which involves obtaining property through coercion and is often associated with fraud.

The most recent criminal-justice data, from Statistics Canada, indicate the police-reported rate of extortion was over four times higher than it was a decade ago. Regional politicians in suburban Toronto and suburban Vancouver have called on Ottawa to redouble efforts against extortion.

In a letter published Thursday to Fintrac's chief executive, Champagne said he expects the agency to work alongside law-enforcement authorities and help investigators with production orders that compel lenders to produce financial records that help build their case.

"I expect [Fintrac] to play an active and central role in combating these urgent threats," Champagne said.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: Atlas Energy Solutions Insider Sells Shares
data.description:
Gregory M Shepard, a shareholder of Atlas Energy Solutions, reported selling a total of 230,000 shares of common stock on February 17 and 18, 2026, for a total sale amount of $2,732,400. The sales were conducted at weighted average prices of $11.75 and $11.98 per share, respectively, with prices ranging from $11.55 to $12.07 for the first transaction and $11.78 to $12.26 for the second. Following these transactions, Shepard directly owns 7,421,210 shares of Atlas Energy Solutions.

data.title: ICE Canola Down Despite Oil Strength
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange declined on Thursday morning, despite rising crude and vegetable oil prices.

Chicago soyoil, European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were up. Crude oil was on the rise due to concerns over an increasing United States military presence in the Middle East. Also, peace talks between Russia and Ukraine ended abruptly on Wednesday.

The Canadian dollar was down more than one-tenth of a U.S. cent compared to Wednesday's close.

Nearly 18,500 contracts were traded. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton as of 8:33 CST:

 
           Price      Change 
Mar       667.80     dn 2.40 
May       680.10     dn 2.60 
Jul       690.60     dn 2.90 
Nov       685.00     dn 2.10

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Canadian Net Trade Looks to Have Contributed to 4Q GDP — Market Talk
data.description: The gains in Canadian export and import volumes in December confirm net trade had a positive affect on 4Q GDP, through volatility behind the numbers suggests export demand remains very weak, Capital Economics' Alexandra Brown says. Exports and imports each rose 1.4% on-month in volume terms, which means exports rose more than 8% annualized in 4Q while imports edged up closer to 1%. Brown says volatile gold swings drove the increase in exports, supported by an another volatile category, aircraft, which surged thanks to big business jet shipments to the U.S. The economist notes the majority of other categories posted monthly declines.(robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

data.title: Morgan Stanley’s EquityZen unit cuts transaction fees for investors, shareholders
data.description:
Morgan Stanley’s wealth management arm said on Thursday that EquityZen has reduced transaction fees for both investors and shareholders on its marketplace.

The lower fees take effect immediately, the bank said, cutting down buy and sell side fees to 2.5% from an earlier 5%.

The investment banking giant closed the acquisition of its private shares platform EquityZen in January.

data.title: CenterPoint profit rises, boosts 10-year spending plan to $65.5 billion
data.description:
By Dharna Bafna

U.S. utility CenterPoint Energy posted a 6.4% rise in fourth-quarter profit on Thursday, and raised its 10-year capital plan as electricity demand climbs across its Houston service territory.

Shares of the company rose nearly 2% in early trading.

Utilities are pouring billions of dollars into capital spending as they field massive requests for new power capacity from Big Tech firms scouring for viable locations for data centers capable of handling complex AI-related tasks.

CenterPoint said it now expects to meet its projected 50% increase in peak power load by 2029, two years ahead of its prior forecast of 2031. It plans to deliver about 10 gigawatts of new load by the end of the decade.

To support the growth, the company raised its 2026–2035 capital plan by $500 million to about $65.5 billion.

CenterPoint also expects at least $10 billion in incremental capital opportunities, including electric transmission, smart meter deployment and data center-related investments in Indiana.

The company expects to keep rates flat through 2028 as incremental load spreads fixed costs across a wider customer base, CEO Jason Wells said on a call.

"The more that we bring, these large projects into our region, they not only help with the tax base in this region...they help us keep our rates affordable," Wells said.

The ballooning electricity demand from data centers is pushing power consumption to record highs and prompting utilities to ramp up spending on infrastructure.

The Houston, Texas-based company posted a profit of $264 million for the three months ended December 31, up from $248 million, the prior year.

On an adjusted basis, the profit of 45 cents met the average of analysts' expectations, according to data combined by LSEG.

The company also reaffirmed its 2026 adjusted profit of $1.89 to $1.91 per share.

data.title: Office building in Sandvika to be refurbished
data.description:
HENT AS, a subsidiary of Sentia, has signed a contract with Kalk Eiendom AS for the refurbishing of Rådhuskvartalet property in Sandvika. The contract value is approximately NOK 125 million, excluding VAT.

Located in the centre of Sandvika, Rådhuskvartalet project involves the transformation of a historic building into new commercial premises for office and food and beverage purposes, as part of a broader ambition to create increased urban activity, vibrancy and quality.

HENT and Kalk Eiendom have successfully completed a collaborative pre-construction phase and will now proceed with the refurbishing of the approximately 10,500 sqm (GBA) building. The project will be carried out in an operational building, with varying scope of works across different areas.

-This project aligns well with HENT’s focus on refurbishing, and we look forward to continuing our collaboration with Kalk Eiendom, says Sentia CEO Jan Jahren.

Construction is scheduled to commence in March 2026, with an estimated build period of approximately one year.

For more information, contact:

Sverre Hærem

EVP & CFO Sentia ASA

+47 952 45 167 |sh@sentiagruppen.com

Iven Opsahl Jebsen

EVP & CCO Sentia ASA

data.description:
RNS Number : 7218T Treatt PLC 19 February 2026  

19 January 2026

TREATT PLC

("Treatt" or "the Company")

Voting Rights and Capital

Treatt's capital consists of 61,283,093 ordinary shares with voting rights. The number of shares held in treasury is currently 1,793,543, the number of shares within the Employee Benefit Trust and Treatt SIP Trust, is 454,418.  Voting rights are waived on treasury shares and shares held within the Employee Benefit Trust and Treatt SIP Trust.

Therefore, the total number of voting rights in Treatt is 59,035,132.

The above figure (59,035,132) may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest in, Treatt under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
